subject: refactor(types): runtime-validate icon hashes + drop dead casts
body:
Three type-hygiene items from the `types branch` trigger:

1. hooks/usePWAInstall.ts: removed two dead-weight casts.
   `(window as Window & { __pwaPromptReceived?: boolean })` and
   `(navigator as Navigator & { standalone?: boolean })` were both
   made redundant by the `types/rn-web-style.d.ts` module augmentations
   in commit d314346 — those ambient declarations already add the
   properties to the base Window/Navigator types. Pre-existing casts
   became dead weight once my own infrastructure landed; closing the
   loop.

2. app/+html.tsx: replaced the bare `JSON.parse(raw) as Record<string,
   string>` cast with a `parseIconHashes()` helper that validates the
   parsed shape at runtime. Top-level must be a JSON object (not
   null/array/primitive); every value must be a string. Throws with
   a specific "got <actualType>" error + remediation (`Regenerate
   with node scripts/inject-icon-hashes.mjs`) if either check fails.
   Catches the "someone hand-edited icon-hashes.json to an invalid
   shape" case that a bare type assertion would miss — the failure
   would otherwise surface as a cryptic downstream `iconUrl()` throw.

3. components/debug/DebugPill.tsx: extracted the per-source color
   override map out of the PALETTE object literal. The inline
   `{ ... } as Record<string, string>` inside `as const` was a
   self-contradicting cast (outer wants literal keys, inner wants
   widened-string keys). Moved to a dedicated top-level
   `SOURCE_PALETTE: Record<string, string> = { ... }` that uses
   variable-level type annotation instead of assertion. Callers
   updated; no semantic change.

Verification:
- npx tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
- npm run verify:inline-scripts: 47 passed, 0 failed
- Unit-tested parseIconHashes against 7 input variants:
  - null → throws "got null"
  - string → throws "got string"
  - number → throws "got number"
  - array → throws "got array"
  - object with number value → throws "value must be a string, got number"
  - object with null value → throws "value must be a string, got null"
  - valid {string: string} → parses correctly
- Expo export with malformed icon-hashes.json aborts the build
  (confirmed via `npx expo export -p web`)

Tags: types, code-quality, pwa, debug
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: fleet-alignment
Semver: patch
